dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9685 IH: No New Fencing At the White House Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Norton introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure

form.official-title: To prohibit the use of Federal, State, foreign, or private funds to install permanent fencing around the White House complex.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the No New Fencing At the White House Act.

section.header: Prohibiting use of funds for permanent fencing around White House complex

subsection.text: No Federal, State, foreign, or private funds may be used to install permanent fencing around the White House complex.
Nothing in subsection (a) shall be construed to prohibit the repair or replacement of fencing that was in existence on the date of enactment of this Act around the White House complex.

paragraph.text: The term White House complex means the Federal land located in Washington, D.C., bound by—

subparagraph.text: H Street Northwest on the north;
Constitution Avenue Northwest on the south; and

paragraph.text: The term State means any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, or any territory or possession or political subdivision thereof.
